transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

ancestor_tree.title: Projection Identifies Exactly the Equivalent Elements
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in A$, \[ \pi(a)=\pi(b)\Longleftrightarrow a\sim b. \]

dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

ancestor_tree.title: Projection Is Surjective
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: The canonical projection $\pi:A\to A/{\sim}$ is surjective.

dependencies.title: Canonical Projection

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

nodes.title: Induced Operation on a Quotient
nodes.statement_display:
If $O:A\times A\to A$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
defines a well-defined binary operation on $A/{\sim}$.
nodes.statement_tex:
If $O:A\times A\to A$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
defines a well-defined binary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Operation Respects an Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $O:A\times A\to A$ be a binary operation and let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The operation $O$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(a\mathbin{O}b\bigr)\sim\bigl(a'\mathbin{O}b'\bigr). \]

direct_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. transitive_dependencies.title: Operation Respects an Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $O:A\times A\to A$ be a binary operation and let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The operation $O$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(a\mathbin{O}b\bigr)\sim\bigl(a'\mathbin{O}b'\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain

ancestor_tree.title: Existence of a Quotient
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

nodes.title: Representative Independence Is Necessary
nodes.statement_display:
If a rule on classes
\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
is well-defined on $A/{\sim}$, then $O$ respects $\sim$.
nodes.statement_tex:

\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
is well-defined on $A/{\sim}$, then $O$ respects $\sim$.

direct_dependencies.title: Operation Respects an Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $O:A\times A\to A$ be a binary operation and let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The operation $O$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(a\mathbin{O}b\bigr)\sim\bigl(a'\mathbin{O}b'\bigr). \]

direct_dependencies.title: Projection Identifies Exactly the Equivalent Elements
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in A$, \[ \pi(a)=\pi(b)\Longleftrightarrow a\sim b. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Induced Operation on a Quotient
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: If $O:A\times A\to A$ respects $\sim$, then \[ [a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b] \] defines a well-defined binary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Operation Respects an Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $O:A\times A\to A$ be a binary operation and let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The operation $O$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(a\mathbin{O}b\bigr)\sim\bigl(a'\mathbin{O}b'\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Projection Identifies Exactly the Equivalent Elements
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For all $a,b\in A$, \[ \pi(a)=\pi(b)\Longleftrightarrow a\sim b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induced Operation on a Quotient
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $O:A\times A\to A$ respects $\sim$, then \[ [a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b] \] defines a well-defined binary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

ancestor_tree.title: Induced Operation on a Quotient
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: If $O:A\times A\to A$ respects $\sim$, then \[ [a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b] \] defines a well-defined binary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

dependencies.title: Operation Respects an Equivalence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $O:A\times A\to A$ be a binary operation and let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The operation $O$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(a\mathbin{O}b\bigr)\sim\bigl(a'\mathbin{O}b'\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset

dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

nodes.title: Induced Predicate on a Quotient
nodes.statement_display:
If $P$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
\overline{P}([a]):\Longleftrightarrow P(a)
\]
defines a well-defined predicate on $A/{\sim}$.
nodes.statement_tex:
If $P$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
\overline{P}([a]):\Longleftrightarrow P(a)
\]
defines a well-defined predicate on $A/{\sim}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Predicate Respects an Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $P$ be a predicate on $A$. The predicate $P$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a'\in A,\, a\sim a'\Longrightarrow \bigl(P(a)\Longleftrightarrow P(a')\bigr). \]

direct_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations

transitive_dependencies.title: Constant Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{constant symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(0\). A constant symbol may occur as a term without taking input terms.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{formula} is an expression formed by the formula grammar of \(\mathcal L\). The grammar starts from atomic formulas and closes under the logical connectives and quantifiers of the language.

transitive_dependencies.title: Function Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{function symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(f\) has arity \(n\), then it forms terms by the pattern \[ f(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Open Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{open formula} is a well-formed formula with at least one free variable. When the free variables are among \(x_1,\ldots,x_n\), it may be displayed as \(\varphi(x_1,\ldots,x_n)\).

transitive_dependencies.title: Predicate Respects an Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $P$ be a predicate on $A$. The predicate $P$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a'\in A,\, a\sim a'\Longrightarrow \bigl(P(a)\Longleftrightarrow P(a')\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Predicate Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{predicate symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(P\) has arity \(n\), then it forms atomic formulas by the pattern \[ P(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations

dependencies.title: Open Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{open formula} is a well-formed formula with at least one free variable. When the free variables are among \(x_1,\ldots,x_n\), it may be displayed as \(\varphi(x_1,\ldots,x_n)\).

dependencies.title: Predicate Symbol
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{predicate symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(P\) has arity \(n\), then it forms atomic formulas by the pattern \[ P(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

dependencies.title: Function Symbol
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{function symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(f\) has arity \(n\), then it forms terms by the pattern \[ f(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

dependencies.title: Constant Symbol
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{constant symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(0\). A constant symbol may occur as a term without taking input terms.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Well-Formed Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: The set of \emph{well-formed formulas} of a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), denoted \(\mathsf{Form}_{\mathcal L}\), is the smallest set of strings satisfying: \begin{enumerate} \item every atomic formula is a well-formed formula; \item if \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula, then \(\neg\varphi\) is a well-formed formula; \item if \(\varphi\) and \(\ps...

dependencies.title: Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{formula} is an expression formed by the formula grammar of \(\mathcal L\). The grammar starts from atomic formulas and closes under the logical connectives and quantifiers of the language.

dependencies.title: Language

ancestor_tree.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

nodes.title: Induced Relation on a Quotient
nodes.statement_display:
If $R$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
\overline{R}([a],[b]):\Longleftrightarrow R(a,b)
\]
defines a well-defined binary relation on $A/{\sim}$.
nodes.statement_tex:
If $R$ respects $\sim$, then
\[
\overline{R}([a],[b]):\Longleftrightarrow R(a,b)
\]
defines a well-defined binary relation on $A/{\sim}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Relation Respects an Equivalence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be a binary relation on $A$. The relation $R$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a',b,b'\in A,\, \bigl(a\sim a'\land b\sim b'\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl(R(a,b)\Longleftrightarrow R(a',b')\bigr). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

nodes.title: Induced Unary Operation
nodes.statement_display:
Let $f:A\to A$. If
\[
a\sim a'\Longrightarrow f(a)\sim f(a'),
\]
then
\[
\overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)]
\]
defines a well-defined unary operation on $A/{\sim}$.
nodes.statement_tex:

\[
a\sim a'\Longrightarrow f(a)\sim f(a'),
\]
then
\[
\overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)]
\]
defines a well-defined unary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Quotient Set
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

nodes.title: Induced Partial Operation on a Quotient
nodes.statement_display:
Let $D\subseteq A$ be saturated under $\sim$, meaning
\[
a\in D\land a\sim a'\Longrightarrow a'\in D.
\]
If $f:D\to A$ respects $\sim$ on $D$, then
\[
\overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)]
\]
is a well-defined partial operation on $A/{\sim}$ with domain
\[
D/{\sim}:=\{[a]\in A/{\sim}:a\in D\}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:

\[
a\in D\land a\sim a'\Longrightarrow a'\in D.

\[
\overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)]
\]
is a well-defined partial operation on $A/{\sim}$ with domain
\[
D/{\sim}:=\{[a]\in A/{\sim}:a\in D\}.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Induced Predicate on a Quotient
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: If $P$ respects $\sim$, then \[ \overline{P}([a]):\Longleftrightarrow P(a) \] defines a well-defined predicate on $A/{\sim}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Induced Unary Operation
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $f:A\to A$. If \[ a\sim a'\Longrightarrow f(a)\sim f(a'), \] then \[ \overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)] \] defines a well-defined unary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Constant Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{constant symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(0\). A constant symbol may occur as a term without taking input terms.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $R$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. For $a \in A$, the \emph{equivalence class} of $a$ is: \[ [a]_R \;:=\; \{\, x \in A \mid (a,x) \in R \,\}. \] When $R$ is clear from context, we write $[a]$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Class
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{equivalence class} of $a\in A$ is \[ [a] := \{x\in A \mid x\sim a\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{formula} is an expression formed by the formula grammar of \(\mathcal L\). The grammar starts from atomic formulas and closes under the logical connectives and quantifiers of the language.

transitive_dependencies.title: Function Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{function symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(f\) has arity \(n\), then it forms terms by the pattern \[ f(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Open Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{open formula} is a well-formed formula with at least one free variable. When the free variables are among \(x_1,\ldots,x_n\), it may be displayed as \(\varphi(x_1,\ldots,x_n)\).

transitive_dependencies.title: Predicate Respects an Equivalence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $P$ be a predicate on $A$. The predicate $P$ \emph{respects} $\sim$ if \[ \forall a,a'\in A,\, a\sim a'\Longrightarrow \bigl(P(a)\Longleftrightarrow P(a')\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Predicate Symbol
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), an \(n\)-ary \emph{predicate symbol} is a non-logical symbol of arity \(n\geq 1\). If \(P\) has arity \(n\), then it forms atomic formulas by the pattern \[ P(t_1,\ldots,t_n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Quotient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Symmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Equivalence Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ be a set. A relation $\sim$ on $A$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

ancestor_tree.title: Induced Unary Operation
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $f:A\to A$. If \[ a\sim a'\Longrightarrow f(a)\sim f(a'), \] then \[ \overline{f}([a]):=[f(a)] \] defines a well-defined unary operation on $A/{\sim}$.

dependencies.title: Representatives Related iff Classes Equal
dependencies.statement_preview: \[ a\sim b \Longleftrightarrow [a]=[b]. \]

dependencies.title: Equivalence Classes Partition the Domain
dependencies.statement_preview: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on $A$ are nonempty, cover $A$, and are pairwise disjoint.

dependencies.title: Existence of a Quotient
dependencies.statement_preview: For any equivalence relation $\sim$ on $A$, the quotient set $A/{\sim}$ exists as the set of equivalence classes, and the canonical projection \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim} \] is well-defined.

dependencies.title: Canonical Projection
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. The \emph{canonical projection} is the map \[ \pi:A\to A/{\sim}, \qquad \pi(a):=[a]. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{quotient set} of $A$ by $R$ is the set of all equivalence classes: \[ A / R \;:=\; \{\, [a]_R \mid a \in A \,\}. \]
